evals.prompt: Can you give me a full company intelligence report on Apple? I want to understand how their earnings sentiment has been trending, how the stock is doing vs competitors, and what analysts are saying.
evals.expected_output: A full company intelligence report plan/outline that: (1) runs getCompanyDescription + getCompanyCompetitors in parallel to get companyId and competitor IDs, (2) runs getAnalytics separately for each of the past 4 quarters using individual documentIDs, (3) builds a quarter-over-quarter comparison table with RISING/FALLING labels for sentiment, investment score, and stock reaction, (4) includes speaker analysis comparing executives vs analysts, (5) produces a structured 11-section report with an Executive Summary that states sentiment direction explicitly.

assertions.name: parallel_foundation_batch
assertions.description: getCompanyDescription and getCompanyCompetitors are called in the same batch (parallel)

assertions.name: per_quarter_analytics
assertions.description: getAnalytics is called 4 times separately (once per earnings call) with individual documentIDs, not one aggregate call

assertions.name: rising_falling_verdict
assertions.description: The plan/report explicitly states sentiment is RISING or FALLING with before/after values

assertions.name: exec_vs_analyst_gap
assertions.description: The plan includes comparing executive sentiment vs analyst sentiment with a gap verdict

assertions.name: full_report_template_selected
assertions.description: The Full Report template is selected (not Quick or other)

assertions.name: chart_html_generated
assertions.description: The plan includes generating an HTML charts file using Chart.js

evals.prompt: Give me a quick overview of Tesla right now — just the headlines on stock performance, financials, and sentiment. I'm in a meeting in 10 minutes.
evals.expected_output: A Quick Report plan that: (1) selects the Quick Report template (~13 tool calls), (2) skips competitors, per-quarter sentiment breakdown, speakers, and charts, (3) runs getCompanyDescription → getStockPrices + getStockChange ×3 + getPredictions ×6 + getAnalytics (scores only) in parallel, (4) produces only Sections 1–3 (Executive Summary, Stock, Financial Outlook).

assertions.name: quick_template_selected
assertions.description: Quick Report template is selected (not Full Report)

assertions.name: under_15_tool_calls
assertions.description: The plan uses approximately 13 tool calls, not 40

assertions.name: no_per_quarter_analytics
assertions.description: getAnalytics is called only once for overall scores (not 4 separate per-quarter calls)

assertions.name: sections_1_to_3_only
assertions.description: Only Sections 1–3 are produced (Executive Summary, Stock Performance, Financial Outlook)

assertions.name: parallel_batch_2_calls
assertions.description: Stock, predictions, and analytics calls are batched in parallel in Batch 2

evals.prompt: How does Microsoft stack up against its competitors? I want to see how MSFT's stock has performed relative to the peer group and S&P 500 over the past year.
evals.expected_output: A Competitive Report plan that: (1) selects Competitive Report template (~15 tool calls), (2) calls getCompanyCompetitors to get competitor companyIds, (3) calls getDeepResearchStockAverage with competitor IDs and includeSp500: true, (4) calls getStockChange per competitor for individual comparison, (5) focuses on Section 9 (Competitive Landscape) plus Section 2 (Stock Performance), and (6) notes MSFT's ranking vs peers.

assertions.name: competitive_template_selected
assertions.description: Competitive Report template is selected (not Full Report)

assertions.name: get_competitors_called
assertions.description: getCompanyCompetitors is called to get competitor companyIds

assertions.name: deep_research_stock_average
assertions.description: getDeepResearchStockAverage is called with competitor companyIds and includeSp500: true

assertions.name: stock_change_per_competitor
assertions.description: getStockChange is called individually per competitor (in parallel)

assertions.name: competitive_section_present
assertions.description: The output includes a competitive landscape / peer comparison section
